item: Acceleration of development pace and productivity
summary: Shortens bounded development activities such as boilerplate generation, test scaffolding, refactoring suggestions, documentation updates, and routine debugging.
related_agent_types: Passive Questioning; Reactive Questioning; Proactive Team-member
tradeoffs_or_questions: Speed may be offset by downstream review, maintenance, integration, or security costs.
evidence_to_collect: Task completion time; defect rate; review effort; maintainability; cognitive load

summary: Connects repositories, issue trackers, CI/CD pipelines, documentation, communication channels, and dashboards.
related_agent_types: Reactive Team-member; Reactive Manager; Proactive Manager
tradeoffs_or_questions: Coordination may become opaque if humans cannot understand escalations or reprioritization.
evidence_to_collect: Coordination breakdowns; handoff delays; duplicated work; shared mental model quality

item: Accessibility of development and SE education
summary: Lowers entry barriers through conversational interaction, examples, and step-by-step scaffolding.
tradeoffs_or_questions: Accessible support may become superficial learning if outputs are accepted without understanding.
evidence_to_collect: Learning gains; transfer tasks; anxiety; motivation; retention

summary: Translates between domain language, requirements, code, tests, and architecture decisions.
tradeoffs_or_questions: Mediation can oversimplify disagreements or hide unresolved ambiguity.
evidence_to_collect: Stakeholder alignment; ambiguity resolution; communication quality

summary: Offers immediate assistance, explains errors, and helps developers recover from blockers.
tradeoffs_or_questions: Simulated support should not replace human care, mentoring, or belonging.
evidence_to_collect: Perceived support; frustration; burnout indicators; help-seeking behavior

summary: Provides contextual explanations, alternatives, examples, and clarification questions during work.
tradeoffs_or_questions: Developers may depend on agent explanations rather than develop diagnostic skill.
evidence_to_collect: Explanation quality; self-efficacy; debugging skill; justification ability

summary: Offloads repetitive or administrative work so developers can focus on architecture and design trade-offs.
tradeoffs_or_questions: Too much offloading can weaken tacit knowledge and situational awareness.
evidence_to_collect: Developer time allocation; perceived creativity; design quality; review depth

summary: Empathy may be treated as an interface feature rather than a human team competence.
tradeoffs_or_questions: Relational work sustaining collaboration may be weakened.
evidence_to_collect: Team climate; perceived empathy; conflict resolution quality

summary: Manual trial, error, debugging, and incremental design may become less central.
tradeoffs_or_questions: Foundational learning practices may be bypassed.
evidence_to_collect: Curriculum outcomes; foundational skill retention; learning-by-doing measures

summary: Agent diagnosis and repair may reduce collaborative debugging and peer learning.
tradeoffs_or_questions: Debugging may become less educational if explanations are weak.
evidence_to_collect: Peer debugging frequency; shared understanding; repair quality

summary: Centralized agent retrieval may reduce informal knowledge exchange among humans.
tradeoffs_or_questions: Teams may lose opportunities to build shared context through conversation.
evidence_to_collect: Knowledge sharing events; network analysis; onboarding effectiveness

item: Human-peer mentoring and ad hoc communication
summary: Novices may ask agents rather than colleagues, reducing mentoring opportunities.
tradeoffs_or_questions: Agents cannot fully reproduce context, judgment, and social support.
evidence_to_collect: Mentoring frequency; novice confidence; social integration

summary: Agentic support can remove productive friction that helps developers learn.
tradeoffs_or_questions: Some friction is valuable for forming mental models and understanding constraints.
evidence_to_collect: Error reflection logs; transfer performance; diagnostic reasoning

summary: Human-AI pair programming retrieves pair work, but with a non-human partner.
tradeoffs_or_questions: Turn-taking, feedback, trust, and accountability change.
evidence_to_collect: Collaboration quality; code review outcomes; perceived partner role

summary: Development becomes conversational: requirements, explanations, design, and code transformations are negotiated through dialogue.
tradeoffs_or_questions: Dialogue can become prompt compliance if disagreement is not supported.
evidence_to_collect: Dialogue quality; clarification rate; negotiated decisions

summary: Agents adapt suggestions to conventions, preferences, and task context.
tradeoffs_or_questions: Personalization may conflict with team standards or introduce inconsistency.
evidence_to_collect: Conformance to conventions; developer satisfaction; variability of outputs

summary: Routine offloading may return human attention to system-level reasoning and quality attributes.
tradeoffs_or_questions: Architectural authority and responsibility must remain clear.
evidence_to_collect: Architecture decision quality; traceability; review depth

summary: Delegation, monitoring, escalation, and role allocation become explicit design problems.
tradeoffs_or_questions: Coordination patterns may reproduce undesirable management dynamics.
evidence_to_collect: Escalation quality; role clarity; intervention frequency

summary: Prompting and requirements formulation converge around intent, constraints, examples, and acceptance criteria.
tradeoffs_or_questions: Requirements may become underspecified if prompts are treated as sufficient.
evidence_to_collect: Requirement quality; ambiguity count; acceptance criteria coverage

summary: Agents may retrieve hands-on validation through generated tests, simulations, and examples.
tradeoffs_or_questions: Practical checks can be mistaken for formal assurance.
evidence_to_collect: Test adequacy; defect detection; validation traceability

summary: When agents shape reasoning too strongly, teams may converge on agent-framed interpretations.
tradeoffs_or_questions: Diversity of thought and critical challenge may decline.
evidence_to_collect: Alternative generation; dissent frequency; decision diversity

summary: Agent mediation may weaken mentoring, informal support, and human care.
tradeoffs_or_questions: Care work can become invisible or undervalued.
evidence_to_collect: Mentoring networks; psychological safety; perceived support

summary: Agentic acceleration may intensify expectations for speed and availability.
tradeoffs_or_questions: Productivity gains may become workload pressure.
evidence_to_collect: Workload; pace expectations; burnout indicators

summary: Repeated delegation may reduce independent reasoning and diagnostic skill.
tradeoffs_or_questions: Support becomes harmful when it replaces learning and judgment.
evidence_to_collect: Skill retention; unaided task performance; reliance measures

summary: Managerial agents may affect task allocation, credit, and evaluation.
tradeoffs_or_questions: Opacity can create fairness and attribution problems.
evidence_to_collect: Workload distribution; credit attribution; fairness perceptions

summary: Automation may hide context needed to build common understanding.
tradeoffs_or_questions: Teams may coordinate efficiently without understanding why.
evidence_to_collect: Shared mental model measures; situation awareness; coordination errors

summary: Higher autonomy can obscure causal chains and responsibility.
tradeoffs_or_questions: Traceability and contestability become mandatory governance requirements.
evidence_to_collect: Audit trails; responsibility assignment; contestation outcomes
